eyebrow.zh: 数字公民破解 · 5 年级

chrome.sub: A new post is ready to go online — but wait. Some of it is safe to share, and some of it should stay private. Read the clues, solve four locks, and lock down what belongs to you.
chrome.briefP: Everything you post online can be seen, saved, and shared by others. Your job is to learn which details are safe to share and which private facts — like your home address, your passwords, and where you are right now — should stay locked away. Crack all four locks, and each one will show the reason its answer is correct.
chrome.footerText: Digital Citizenship Breakouts · Runs entirely in your browser — no logins, no data collected.
chrome.winH: You locked down your private info!
chrome.winP: You now know which details are safe to share, how to check who can see your posts, why a location can wait, and which private facts must stay secret. Smart and safe posting!

clues.title: What is okay to post?
clues.body: Some details are safe to share online. Your first name, a hobby you love, or a favorite book can be posted without giving away who you are or where to find you. These small facts do not put you in danger.

clues.title: What should stay secret?
clues.body: Some information is private and should never be posted. Your home address, the name of your school, your phone number, and your passwords can let a stranger find you or take over your accounts. Keep these locked away.

clues.title: Who can see this?
clues.body: Every app has privacy settings that decide who can see what you post. Before you share, check the settings so that only people you trust — like family and close friends — can view it, instead of the whole internet.

clues.title: Should you post where you are?
clues.body: Posting your exact location tells everyone where to find you right now. A photo tagged 'at the park on Main Street' can be seen by strangers. It is safer to share where you went AFTER you have already left.

clues.title: Who is really watching?
clues.body: When a post is public, your audience is not just your friends — it can be anyone in the world. Always ask 'Who might see this?' before you share, and imagine the biggest possible audience reading it.

clues.title: The science room pet
clues.body: The fifth-grade science room has a pet gecko named Comet who suns himself under a warm lamp. Comet only eats crickets on Tuesdays. This is a fun fact, but it will not help you open any lock.

locks.title: Keep or Share?
locks.q: Check EVERY detail that should stay PRIVATE and NOT be posted.

items.t: The password to your game account
The name of the school you attend

locks.reason: Private facts like your address, passwords, and school can let a stranger find you or break into your accounts, so they stay secret — but a favorite food or book is safe to share.

locks.title: Safe to Post
locks.q: Which post is SAFE to share online?
options: I live at 412 Oak Lane — come visit anytime after school!
Hi, I'm Sam and I love drawing dragons and reading comics!
My phone number is on my profile, text me to hang out at Lincoln Elementary.
locks.reason: Sharing just a first name and a hobby is safe, but posting your address, phone number, or school gives strangers the details they need to find you.

locks.title: One Question First
locks.q: Before you post, ask 'Who can ____ this?' What word fills the blank? (Hint: it means to view something with your eyes.)
locks.reason: Before you share anything, ask 'Who can SEE this?' — thinking about your audience helps you keep private things private.

locks.title: The Safe Way to Post
locks.q: You want to post a photo from the park. Tap the steps in the RIGHT order.

pads.e: 🚶 Wait until you leave the park

locks.reason: First wait until you LEAVE so you are not sharing your live location, then CHECK your settings to control who sees it, and only then POST the photo safely.
